scenario: A survey form asks "Which features do you use?" with multiple selectable answers from a fixed list. Each selection should be independently toggleable; result should be a list of selected values.
question: Which Forms component fits?

options.text: Radio component -- a set of mutually exclusive options displayed as radio buttons. A Radio component allows the user to select exactly one option at a time from the available choices. Selecting one option automatically deselects the previously selected one. For a survey where users can independently select multiple features simultaneously, a single-select Radio component cannot capture multi-item responses.

Individual single Checkbox components -- one per feature option, each bound to a separate boolean process variable. Using multiple individual Checkbox elements technically allows selecting multiple options simultaneously but requires manual assembly of the selected values into a list via output mappings after form submission. The Checkbox group component is the canonical multi-select alternative that automatically produces a list of selected values.

Select (Dropdown) component -- a dropdown menu that allows the user to choose one option from a list. The Select component is single-select by design, presenting options in a dropdown and storing exactly one selected value. For a multi-selection survey question where users can choose any combination of applicable features, a single-select dropdown component cannot satisfy the multi-choice requirement.

Checkbox group component -- provides a set of checkbox options from a defined list, enabling multi-selection. Each option is independently toggleable. The bound process variable is of type array and stores the values of all currently selected options. When no option is selected, the array is empty. For small fixed-option datasets like feature lists, Checkbox group is the canonical Camunda 8 Forms multi-select component.

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda Forms 8.8 element library names this component Checkbox group (per forms-element-library-checkbox-group.md:3 -- "A set of checkbox options providing data multi-selection for small datasets"). Bound to data of type any[] (recommended string[]); the component correlates the bound list with selected option values (:24-41). Note: the doc image is incidentally labelled "Checklist Selection" but the formal element name is Checkbox group. For larger datasets, Taglist is the alternative (multi-select from large lists).

c.text: Incorrect. Multiple individual Checkbox elements work but require manual list assembly via output mapping; Checkbox group is the canonical multi-select.

d.text: Correct. Select component is single-select by design.

explanation: Select component is single-select by design.
